{"query":"xz utils core components license change replaced public domain current license","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1700,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://tukaani.org/xz/","title":"XZ Utils","content":"XZ Utils are a complete C99 implementation of the .xz file format.
The core of the XZ Utils compression code is based on LZMA SDK but it has been modified significantly to be suitable for XZ Utils.
liblzma is a compression library with an API similar to that of zlib.
xz is a command line tool with syntax similar to that of gzip.
CVE-2026-34743: Buffer overflow in lzma_index_append()
In XZ Utils 5.3.3alpha to 5.8.0, the multithreaded .xz decoder in liblzma has a bug where invalid input can at least result in a crash.
When built for native Windows (mingw-w64 or MSVC), the command line tools from XZ Utils 5.6.2 and older have a command line argument injection vulnerability.
Command line tools built for Cygwin or MSYS2 are unaffected. liblzma is unaffected.
XZ Utils 5.6.0 (2024-02-24) and 5.6.1 (2024-03-09) release tarballs contain a backdoor that was inserted by a malicious co-maintainer.
It was discovered by Andres Freund and made public on 2024-03-29.
The incident is known as CVE-2024-3094.
See the XZ Utils backdoor page for more information and also the XZ Utils review notes.
A patch to fix it was made public on 2022-04-07.
See the NEWS file for a summary of changes between versions.
XZ Utils source packages are also available on Codeberg and Sourceforge.
CMake-based build became feature complete in June 2024.
From the version 5.5.2beta onwards, the core components of XZ Utils are under the BSD Zero Clause License (0BSD).
See the file COPYING for more details.
See also the xz (command line tools) and xz-man (man pages) domains in the Translation Project."},{"url":"https://www.spec.org/cpu2017/Docs/licenses/XZ-Utils.txt","title":"Xz utils","content":"Different licenses apply to different files in this package.
are in the public domain.
utilities in the debug directory are in the public domain.
that are under various free software licenses.
This software includes code from XZ Utils."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/XZ_Utils","title":"XZ Utils - Wikipedia","content":"XZ Utils (previously LZMA Utils) is a set of free software command-line lossless data compressors, including the programs lzma and xz, for Unix-like operating systems and, from version 5.0 onwards, Microsoft Windows.
XZ Utils started as a Unix port of Igor Pavlov's LZMA-SDK that has been adapted to fit seamlessly into Unix environments and their usual structure and behavior.
Development of XZ Utils took place within the Tukaani Project, a small group of developers who once maintained a Linux distribution based on Slackware.
A number of Linux distributions, including Fedora, Slackware, Ubuntu, and Debian use xz for compressing their software packages.
Arch Linux previously used xz to compress packages, but as of 27 December 2019, packages are compressed with Zstandard compression.
Fedora Linux also switched to compressing its RPM packages with Zstandard with Fedora Linux 31.
On 29 March 2024, Andres Freund, a PostgreSQL developer working at Microsoft, announced that he had found a backdoor in XZ Utils, impacting versions 5.6.0 and 5.6.1.
Archived from the original on 24 February 2026.
"Changes/Switch RPMs to zstd compression"."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"xz utils core components license change replaced public domain current license","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.spec.org/cpu2017/Docs/licenses/XZ-Utils.txt","title":"XZ Utils License","content":"Different licenses apply to different files in this package. liblzma is in the public domain. - xz, xzdec, and lzmadec command line tools are in the public","score":0.657297,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.sstinc.co.jp/wp-content/uploads/static/pro3oss/out_html/deb/xz-utils/copyright.html","title":"Untitled","content":"License: Different licenses apply to different files in this package. xz, xzdec, and lzmadec command line tools are in the public domain … documentation files","score":0.60077465,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://github.com/tukaani-project/xz/issues/79","title":"Switch from public domain to BSD Zero Clause License? · Issue #79 · tukaani-project/xz · GitHub","content":"To avoid those issues, a widely-accepted public-domain-equivalent license that requires no attribution is considered for future versions. # Why the XZ projects are in the public domain. Since XZ Utils, XZ Embedded, and XZ for Java are derived from the LZMA SDK code, I felt that it made no sense to have more restrictions on the XZ projects code than what LZMA SDK had. * The Unlicense is a PD dedication with a fallback license. Compared to 0BSD and MIT-0, a PD dedication doesn't seem to add much practical value but it can fuel discussions about how the PD-dedication, fallback license, and warranty disclaimer interact with each other and so on. With the above considerations, my impression is that 0BSD is currently be the best of the well-known public-domain-equivalent licenses for software although the difference to MIT-0 is minuscule. 0BSD to MIT-0 should be fine because there is no PD dedication in MIT-0.).","score":0.5622973,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/XZ_Utils","title":"XZ Utils - Wikipedia","content":"# XZ Utils. **XZ Utils** (previously **LZMA Utils**) is a set of free software command-line lossless data compressors, including the programs lzma and xz, for Unix-like operating systems and, from version 5.0 onwards, Microsoft Windows. XZ Utils can compress and decompress the *xz* and *lzma* file formats. In addition, decompression of the .lz format used by lzip is supported since version 5.3.4. Compression can be much slower than gzip, and is slower than bzip2 for high levels of compression, and is most useful when a compressed file will be used many times. Both the behavior of the software and the properties of the file format have been designed to work similarly to those of the popular Unix compressing tools gzip and bzip2. Version 1.22 or greater of the GNU implementation of tar has transparent support for tarballs compressed with lzma and xz, using the switches `--xz` or `-J` for xz compression, and `--lzma` for LZMA compression.","score":0.52086246,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://cdpstudio.com/manual/cdp-studio/legal/credits-xz.html","title":"xz License | Legal Information","content":"Here is a rough summary of which licenses apply to which parts of this package (but check the individual files to be sure!): - liblzma is in the public domain.Read more","score":0.44102347,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.74,"request_id":"b04e218f-e511-4c91-8fe4-a7fdc48a48c3"}
